compiled_state.title: Tier E review follow-up: close test-coverage gaps (signature close-gate, transition exit-10 parity, compact negative paths)
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
The E0-E4 opus review found the riskiest paths under-covered by the dual-run parity suite. Non-blocking but should be closed before E9 sign-off.

## What
- _signature_gate (story/epic close): zero dual-run parity coverage — the parametrized transition tests only use task tickets. Add story/epic close with verify.require_signature_for_close on/off, a stale-HEAD rejection, and --force-close asserting the warning bytes + audit comment.
- transition exit-10 optimistic-concurrency: no parity test (claim exit-10 IS covered). Add 'transition <id> <wrong-current> closed -> exit 10' dual-run + a library ConcurrencyError assertion. (Cheap: a wrong-current transition on an open ticket is non-mutating.)
- compact negative paths: no parity for lock-timeout / git-failure / compact-all --limit branch.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] signature/force-close close-gate dual-run parity tests (story+epic, gate on/off, stale HEAD, force-close)
- [ ] transition exit-10 parity (CLI byte-parity + library ConcurrencyError)
- [ ] compact-all --limit + compact error-path coverage
- [ ] all new tests green

comments.body: DONE (commit b10a0b16). 13 in-process tests covering the 3 review-flagged gaps (no dual-run — bash is gone): signature close-gate over story+epic (gate off/on, stale-HEAD 'different commit', --force-close warning bytes + FORCE_CLOSE audit comment); transition exit-10 (CLI exit 10 + library ConcurrencyError, store unchanged); compact-all --limit + compact lock-timeout/git-failure error paths (monkeypatched seams, no-corruption assertions). Full suite 1675 passed; ruff+format green. Tests directly inspected for genuineness (real path-exercising assertions, not change-detectors).
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
